question: What is the preferred approach used by Prometheus to collect metrics from a target?
What is the Observability?
correct_answer: Observability: understand what’s happening inside a system and predict how it will behave in the future
incorrect_answers: Monitoring is the ability to understand internal state
Telemetry is the ability to debug a system

question: What is RED Method?
correct_answer: RED Method consists of: (Request) Rate + (Request) Errors + (Request) Duration

question: What are the distinctions between SLO, SLI, and SLA?
correct_answer: SLO: Service Level Objective (Goal), SLA: Service Level Agreement (Contract), SLI: Service Level Indicator (Metrics)
incorrect_answers: SLA is the measured indicator; SLI is the contract; SLO is the penalty clause
SLI is the target; SLO is the measurement; SLA is the internal goal
SLO and SLA are the same; SLI is a log format

question: In the context of tracing, what is the meaning or representation of a span?
correct_answer: Span is a single operation/unit of work within a distributed system and captures the start and end times, duration, and associated metadata of a specific operation

question: In which scenarios is distributed tracing less beneficial or NOT as applicable?
incorrect_answers: In large microservice systems with many hops
When you only have batch jobs and no requests
When you need numeric time-series metrics

question: What are typically tracked within a span of a trace?
correct_answer: Operation Name, Trace ID and Span ID, Start and End Timestamps, Duration, Parent Span ID
incorrect_answers: A complete end-to-end trace across services
A metric sample at one timestamp

question: What is the good and bad metric?
correct_answer: bad: a metric with a lot fo variance and poor correlation with user experience, good: metric to set easier threashold for bcs there is no overlap at all.
incorrect_answers: Good metrics are high-cardinality; bad metrics are low-cardinality
Good metrics are verbose logs; bad metrics are traces
Good metrics are always CPU usage; bad metrics are latency

question: Which type of data is monitored by Prometheus?
In the context of monitoring and observability, what type of data is typically used to define a SLI?
correct_answer: SLI is typically derived from metrics
incorrect_answers: RED Method consists of: (Request) Rate + (Request) Errors + (Request) Duration

question: What is the meaning or purpose of an error budget policy?
correct_answer: An error budget policy is a concept used in the context of SLOs and SLAs and is to define the acceptable level of errors or service disruptions that a system or service can experience within a given time period.

question: What is one advantage of the push model for recoring metrics compared to pull models?
correct_answer: timely and proactive data collection (real-time or near real-time) / pushing into the centralized data system
incorrect_answers: To gather and aggregate textual event data from a service for troubleshooting

question: How do Prometheus, ELK stack, and InfluxDB differ in terms of their functionalities and use cases?
correct_answer: InfluxDB is a pull-based time-series database designed to handle high volumes of time-stamped data (IoT, Sensor, Analytics).
incorrect_answers: Prometheus: logs; ELK: metrics; InfluxDB: tracing
All three are log search stacks
All three are identical TSDBs with PromQL

question: What is the definition of a metric?
correct_answer: ELK stack is a push-based system, used for collecting, processing, storing, and visualizing log data.
incorrect_answers: SLI is typically derived from metrics

question: What are the Prometheus exemplars?
correct_answer: Prometheus is a pull-based time-series database and monitoring system specifically designed for monitoring dynamic cloud-native environments.

question: What is one of the main purposes or goals of logging?
incorrect_answers: SLI is typically derived from metrics

question: What are the 3 core components of observability?
correct_answer: An exemplar is a specific trace representative of measurement taken in a given time interval and provides additional information about a specific data point.

question: What is the Monitoring
correct_answer: To gather and aggregate textual event data from a service for troubleshooting
incorrect_answers: SLI is typically derived from metrics

question: What is the Telemetry?
What is the challenges of observability?

question: What is the CLI utility tool for Prometheus called?

correct_answer: scalability for large-scale deployments with millions of TS, Long-term storage, High cardinality, HA and Replication

question: What is Service Discovery and which categories are there?
correct_answer: SD is a mechanism that allow to automatically discover and monitor targets and services. There are 2 categories: top-down (e.i. ec2) and bottom-up (e.i. consol) mechanisms of static SD
incorrect_answers: A way to store metrics in remote storage
A PromQL function for joining vectors
A method to generate dashboards automatically

question: Which property configures the timing to scrape metrics from targets?
incorrect_answers: how frequently Prometheus collects and updates the metrics

question: Which section in the Prometheus configuration file governs the selection of targets to be scraped?
Which action in the label configuration is used to delete a specific target?

question: How is managed data retention in prometheus?

incorrect_answers: how frequently Prometheus collects and updates the metrics

question: What is required to be able to reload Prometheus?

question: How can you delete the specific time series metrics of Prometheus?
correct_answer: starting the server with the flag --web.enable-admin-api + curl - X POST -g 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/admin/tsdb/delete_series?match[]={xxxx="yyy"}'

question: How can you delete the all time series metrics of Prometheus?
correct_answer: starting the server with the flag --web.enable-admin-api + $ curl -X POST -g 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones'

question: Which format does file-based SD provide?
What is PromQL?
incorrect_answers: A YAML configuration format for Prometheus
A log query language for ELK
An alert routing language for Alertmanager

question: What is histogram metric in Prometheus?
correct_answer: Histogramm samples observations (e.g. request durations or response sizes) and counts them in configurable buckets
incorrect_answers: A single gauge with labels for quantiles
A counter that resets every scrape
A log-derived metric stored as strings

question: Which 4 data types are used in PromQL?
correct_answer: Scalar, String, Instant Vector, Range Vector

question: What is the name of the vector in Prometheus that stores a single sample value?
Which PromQL function is used to estimate the value of a time series at a future time, t seconds from the current time, based on the range vector v?
Between what type of expressions can logical operators be defined?
Which function can be used to calculate the average of a range vector in Prometheus?
What is the diff between avg(...) and avg_over_time(...)?
correct_answer: avg_over_time(...) has range vector as input and returns range vector as output. avg(...) has instant vector as input and returns aggregated number.
incorrect_answers: avg_over_time aggregates across series; avg aggregates over time

question: With which type of metrics is the rate(...) function primarily used in Prometheus?
What does the term “offset” refer to in Prometheus?
correct_answer: offset refers to the past time as duration
